name: phantex
summary:
Local OSINT recon terminal. A Flask-based web dashboard that runs on your
laptop and scans for nearby wireless signals. Start with Bluetooth -- more
modules coming.

documentation.readme: Local OSINT recon terminal. A Flask-based web dashboard that runs on your laptop and scans for nearby wireless signals. Start with Bluetooth -- more modules coming.
usage_sections: Linux with bluez installed (hcitool must be on PATH for classic BT scanning)
A Bluetooth adapter (BLE scanning works without root; classic BT may need CAPNETRAW)
File handler: DEBUG+ -> var/log/phantex.log, 5 MB x 3 backups.
Terminal handler: WARNING+ only via a filter, with one exception
APScheduler is silenced on the terminal (file still captures it).

arguments.name: --app
arguments.description: CLI flag '--app' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

arguments.name: --filter
arguments.description: BPF filter expression for packet selection.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with haxorthematrix/iSniff-GPS-ng (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with thewhiteh4t/seeker (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with smoorthi_uno/Shodan_Python_Project (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web, cloud, iots] in the Reconnaissance category. Fits when the engagement needs: Local OSINT recon terminal. A Flask-based web dashboard that runs on your
laptop and scans for nearby wireless signals. Start with Bluetooth -- more
modules coming.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Local OSINT recon terminal. A Flask-based web dashboard that runs on your laptop and scans for nearby wireless signals. Start with Bluetooth -- more modules coming.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/xransum/phantex; git clone https://github.com/xransum/phantex. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `uv run flask --app phantex.app:create_app run`. Further invocations from upstream: `flask --app phantex.app:create_app run`. Local OSINT recon terminal. A Flask-based web dashboard that runs on your laptop and scans for nearby wireless signals. Start with Bluetooth -- more modules coming.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
